reviews.review_title: Do not buy this crappy game
reviews.review_description: If I could give zero stars I would. I should have read the countless horrible reviews before I purchased this terrible game. Whoever created this game, just created a piece of crap and left it here to die. At least take the game off the store… You cannot skip plays if you do to get yourself back on the field, the entire screen goes black and there’s nothing you can do but quit the game and go back in. And it does it every single time. It’s not just every once in a while. Once you score touchdown, and the two point conversion a loud buzzing sound just goes off in your ear and it never stops. Pushing no combination of buttons fixes this. You cannot play through the storyline at all because you cannot even make it through one touchdown without the game freezing up. Absolutely horrible game would love my money back.

Game used to be fun, never really a story behind it u were just Patrick mahomes. I stopped and playing came back and everytime I would throw the ball my receiver would run backwards. I stopped playing again and came back and now everytime I go in a game and score and go for 2, even if I miss or make the 2pt it just freezes everybody in please. Horrible game

I used to be able to play this game with absolutely no problems then I don’t play it for a while then I get on again and it won’t let me throw the ball and and won’t let me even play the game I can only play practice the screen just goes black I want my money back now

reviews.review_title: What are we doing here man
reviews.review_description: This game is one of the worst sports games I’ve played in recent years. I could use a refund here please
U js pick plays and throw, the throwing mechanics are so horrible and theres not even really a story or any other modes for it. Would give it a 0 star rating but I cant

The game is actually fun when it works.  Better than 2MD football.

But there are game breaking bugs.  The screen goes black when you try to skip a play or when the other team scores.  So the game is basically unplayable.

I hestitate to refund it because the game is fun if you can force it to work.  Maybe they will end up fixing it some day.  Also these licensed games have a history of getting removed from the store so better to buy it anyway and pray that they care enough to fix their game.

not quite what I was expecting. I only played for 30 minutes. There is nothing to click to refund. Not a good experience.

The refund instructions do not work. There is no option to refund.

Update

I never had the option to refund this game. the refund option was never present.
Now I'm stuck with a game I'll never play

reviews.review_title: Trash ! And the game crashes offline!!
reviews.review_description: This game will crash if you play offline ! The developers are not as good as the devs  from 2md football as they have not done any major updates to the game . It still crashes , you can’t run , the physics of the players are ridiculous and stiff , the game looks ok and there is some fun to be had but if your torn between this and 2md football , go with 2md football , this game doesn’t even try , it’s good to be able to see your defense play, but they can’t stop anybody, and the opposing teams quarterback is as stiff as a board and hard to watch , I thought this game would have potential after a few updates , but they didn’t update anything and have seem to go into hiding . Don’t waste your time unless your really desperate for a football game, get 2md or better yet get NFL pro era

personally am not a fan of the throwing mechanic, you get bailed out by receivers because the o-line is worse than joe burrows in superbowl 56

I am rewriting this review.  After NFL Pro Era this seems like playing an 8 bit game.  I hopped back in to play again and it REALLY is cheap looking - the triangles for the players and no even attempt to make it look other than cartoony.

Do NOT waste your $ on this.  It was good way back in the day when there were no alternatives but Pro Era is the game you are looking for if you are looking for football simulation.

So I was hoping this game would have came out worth updates or anything and nothing. I wpuldve fathered played with my home team QB (Lamar Jackson) over and over if I wanted just a game. May you all please update the game to blow our minds with a mega update change. Or this was just one of those............

reviews.review_title: Game Has Barely Changed Since Launch
reviews.review_description:
Edit 1: I am keeping my review of the game and its 3 star rating, but I am shocked by the lack of developer updates. There are very simple fixes that could make this a 5 star game, but without updates and with the high price tag, you might as well just get 2MD.

Edit 2: Having completed a season and winning  "Playoff1" (there is no second playoff game) and then winning a High School bowl game against the same team (you read that correctly), I'm lowering my rating to 2 stars from 3. This game has been out for several months, and there still are almost no updates. 2MD in the same time continues to churn out updates. The high school games and pro games in MVP play almost entirely the same but in venues that look bigger and with even more generic team names. There are some stupid glitches like players catching the ball and running the wrong way. The "forward progress bug" that I've seen devs write about on other reviews is still present and has been a problem since the game came out. Running back plays are still a mess and almost never gain yards.  I bought this game the week it was released, and it has barely changed in that time.  I'm very disappointed.

Original review: This game is a lot of fun. I will keep playing it, but 2MD set a very high standard at a very cheap cost. For all the good that MVP has, it's not as good overall as 2MD and at double the price.

I like the slight arcade feel. It seems appropriate that you have to have time slow down and take a breath to see if your receiver or the other team caught the ball.

The throwing motion seems off. I never quite know if the game will say I threw a long or a bullet pass. They give you a trajectory arc, but a real person's throw isn't really slow enough for that to help.

Playing full games is fun. It's easy to cheese and get the win. Much like Madden and 2MD, you can just keep running the same play. It's fun though. I'd say it's challenging, but  the challenge isn't particularly due to skill. The AI is inconsistent and makes interceptions on plays it shouldn't but then doesn't pick off throws that they should. The AI is plain bizarre. They are pretty dumb at the snap (as in not covering receivers) but they gravitate immediately to a receiver who has the ball coming their way.

The tutorial is worthless. It just tells you how to throw and call audibles. There's a lot that should be mentioned. Calling timeouts or opting to kick are part of your audible screen (on your non-throwing hand). The timeouts don't do very much because you can't access that screen until you're ready to snap the ball anyway. There's no description of how to do a planned run play. I'm fairly sure running plays (which are almost always a loss of yards) are just counted as short passes. I don't think I've ever seen the opposing team do a run.

Speaking of running, why would you not allow your QB to run? It's one of Patrick Mahomes's skillsets. Honestly, a lot of the stuff I mentioned above is forgiveable because the game is new and you end up figuring some of it out along the way, but not being able to move when there is open field in front of you is a pretty glaring hole that should be an easy fix. 2MD has it, so surely the devs are aware.

I'd still recommend the game to anyone. It's just not a 5 star experience right now. Make a few updates and it's a near-perfect experience.

developer_response.body: Hi Mr. Dilligaf - We launched in October and have already made an update. We will continue to make more as we hear from others. Thank you for your feedback. We're glad you're enjoying it so far. If you need to give us more specific feedback, our team would love to hear from you: publishing@citylightsvr.com.

reviews.review_description: I'm giving this game five stars because it is trying to do something different in a sports game. I'm sure future updates will change a lot of the issues. This is a fresh takeaway from the 2md football game.  One game you can pretty much stand still and play or run around .The other allows you to use the joystick. One game is fun for a two minute huddle and the other is just a fun full football game. I believe between these two games having both of them is the way to go. I don't want the same game twice.   I like having a different reason to play one game or the other. This being said,  good work developers. Keep me sending us updates.  Don't try to be like 2md . Be your game and keep it fresh and different.

developer_response.body: We love to hear it! Thank you for all the feedback!

reviews.review_description:
I want to start this review on a positive note and say that I've been having a lot of fun overall with this game. It has a license to use Mahomes likeness and it really differentiates itself from its main competitor 2MD by having full games and more robust stat tracking.

It also has more detailed player models with shadows and a more intuitive audible system than 2MD. So it has a few key differences that I think help it standout. Unfortunately it also has some huge steps back as well.

First and most obvious by now is that you cannot move with the joystick. To avoid pressure you must always physically step away from the defender. You also then cannot scramble or run a QB draw for instance. Adding onto the issues is that you never feel totally in control of your pass even with all assists off. I'll try to lob a ball and sometimes it will throw a laser despite my arc and loft of my arm. 

That issue there is the Achilles heel to its primary competitor. If I could turn off the arcade aspects I think I'd like it a bit more. Furthermore over the years 2MD allowed you to take control of the receiver after the reception. If this game wanted to standout it would at least offer that as well and maybe go one beyond it and do what Sports Challenge does with you teleporting to the receiver to make the reception. It's seriously a generation behind 2MD in gameplay aspects.

It has potential and I won't be refunding it because I do enjoy it. It runs well on both Quest models, you can resume your game if you have to step away, and playing full games definitely is a step in the right direction. They just need to take the shackles off the player and let us use the joystick to scramble, allow us to toggle the arcade elements, and at least allow us to take control of the receiver like 2MD and they'd have a winner. As it stands now it's $5 more than 2MD and not as good.

Edit: I've found a few other issues and one I think is a bug.

1. It doesn't seem to consider forward progress. The ball is placed where the receiver is tackled and not where the contact began. I've seen several obvious first downs driven back and not considering forward progress.

2. Runs and screen plays are rarely successful. Almost always the receivers don't block on a screen and runs are typically stuffed immediately. I've tried making better pitches to ensure the back has momentum and even bellied out to simulate a handoff (still comes across as a short pitch). Due to the forward progress bug it's almost always a 5 yard loss or worse.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review and feedback! We are passing along all of this to the developers with the forward progress bug you mentioned at the top of the list.

reviews.review_description: So I picked this up after playing the other football game just to see and it has some fun aspects. I like the play calling and throwing but I wish I could move and run. Kicking FG’s and better realistic graphics would be more immersive. However, all of the potential fun was lost after it froze up in the 3rd qtr of my first game. 🤬

developer_response.body: We hate to hear that it froze! You can reach us at publishing@citylightsvr.com if you're still having this problem. Our team can help.

reviews.review_description:
Overall, this game is actually really good! The player animations are solid, the throwing mechanics are responsive, gameplay is immersive, and the fact that you get to play  full games and watch the kick off in addition to watching your team's defense play is actually pretty awesome. 

There is still some room for improvement though. 

First (and a big one. Hope the devs add this in a future update), unlike 2MD Football, there's no free movement. You snap and throw the ball from the same spot. If you see the defensive trying to sack you, you can't go anywhere. 

Second, although I like the artistic direction, I feel like they could do a little more with the stadium and audience. 

Third, I get that they're trying to make it more of an arcade style experience, but the sounds when completing a pass are a little too arcady.. 

fourth, this is more of a "hope they add this" rather than an opportunity. It'd be sweet if they added multiplayer. For example: When playing defense, you just pick the defensive formation while the other player is playing quarter back calling the offensive plays. Could be awesome. 

I think this game has a ton of potential. Between dialing back the arcade feel, focus on adding multiplayer, and quarterback movement, I think this could be amazing.


Anyway, setting all of that aside, this game is still awesome. Definitely recommend it

developer_response.body: Thank you for your thoughtful and thorough review. We are always looking to improve our game and will take your feedback into consideration. Follow us on social media @CityLightsVR for updates!

reviews.review_description: I’m a fan of patty so this game is for me and I really like it

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We appreciate your concerns, and will be updating the game as we go. Follow our social channels @CityLightsVR for updates!

reviews.review_description:
Updated review:  My first crack at this game had me thinking it was crap.  I decided to give it a second shot and I have the following notes that may help new players:

When the game slows down, pay attention to the in game graphics, they show you the route the receiver you're looking at is running and where to throw to (unless you turn that off).  When the world slows down you may forget to lead your receiver like you would in real time.

Also, remember you can't use the joystick but you can dodge defenders using roomscale. The game doesn't explain this but it is very fun when you pull it off. 

In settings there is a help menu that explains a lot that should really be in the tutorial. Throw power by default is a mix of how much you move your arm and how hard you throw, turning the power assist off makes this purely how hard you throw.  The angle and throwing motion you use makes a big difference and you should practice because a tiny difference when under pressure makes the ball do completely different things. 

The accuracy assist should be left on default for most, it seems to make things go the way they should, especially on short plays, but it only seems to work if you can get the player route highlighted in game (I cant figure out how this happens, by gaze or arm direction?)  It is really obvious on shovel passes to the running back and if you shovel it without this active it can often bounce off them.

You can make custom plays and doing this helped me to understand what the symbols mean at the end if a route.  Overall the editor is a bit harder to use than 2md but is probably a bit more powerful?

I think 2md has a more solid overall set of base gameplay mechanics and better graphics (now, but not when that game first launched!) but after taking my time to understand what the game is trying to do it isn't that bad and can be a lot of fun.  Having a proper season and some stat tracking and full games makes it feel more like a football game and less like a football mini-game.

developer_response.body: UPDATED: We're so happy to hear that you gave it a second chance and are enjoying it, Shawn!

reviews.review_description: Having so much fun with it! Blowing my mind, quite frankly. Don't understand how anyone could give this less than 4 stars, to each their own I guess. Get it!

developer_response.body: Amazing, brainchild! We're happy to hear it!

reviews.review_description:
I know people love Quest 2 software, but unfortunately a not small number of it is hot garbage.
NOT SO with "MVP Football" [MVPF].  Not only is this the only full football game on Quest 2 -- the arcadey "2MD Football" obviously doesn't count -- but it's one of a literal handful of titles that make you appreciate just how magical VR is in general and the Quest 2 is in particular.  In MVPF, you start in High School and go all the way up to the Pros.  And during a game when your team's on Defense, you get to watch the entire action from your sideline, just like real football, while your wrist computer offers play-by-play in the form of easy-to-understand text messages!  Although there are a few tweaks here & there MVPF could use, the *foundation* of it is so solid, I gave it five stars anyway.  The tweaks can easily come in an update.  

What could be improved includes:
> CROWDS: Even a .JPEG of people would be better than just multicolored squares.  lol
> QB MOBILITY: A football sim where the QB can't move ...??  QBs run for yardage too, y'know!
> DIFFERENT STADIUMS: Not crucial, but it'd SO help with the immersion.
> TOGGLING PLAYBOOK: A button press on your playbook hand would remove that distraction.
> CUSTOMIZATION: Coloring your Home/Away uniforms & naming your players also improves  immersion.
> MUSIC: Not every football fan enoys rap.  How about the classic football orchestral soundtracks?

Again, despite these little niggling, MVPF is terriffic!  Pick it up today!
OMAHA!!  OMAHA!!  🙂👍🏻

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for leaving such a thoughtful review, Cary! We agree that these are great suggestions and are passing along to the developers. Please follow us on Twitter for updates: @MVPFootballVR.

reviews.review_description: Great it’s a great game. I like it but if you could scramble it would be a 5 star

developer_response.body: Thanks, Griffin! We'll pass this along to our team of developers.

reviews.review_title: Pls Add Free Loco & POV For Runners!
reviews.review_description:
With superb player models & animations, you can finally play a FULL game and watch your defence battle for you as well! The potential here is HUGE, but there’s ONE pretty glaring issue that needs to be addressed: MVP Football’s direct rival allows you to move around the pocket/field, to avoid tacklers and to also gain rushing yards. The irony here, is considering that Patrick Mahomes is one of the most dynamic and mobile quarterbacks in the NFL today, you yourself as a player in the Mahomes Experience, cannot move from your fixed position at all with the thumb sticks - you have physically move within your own play space. That’s cool, if you have acres of room, but seeing as many(me included) have very limited space or would prefer to play seated(!), in some ways, this is more the Tom Brady Experience, than Mahomes!

All jokes aside, if the devs can add free locomotion and POV for all ball-carriers, and perhaps even lean more towards the simulation side(with some linebacker/free safety defensive play?), then I might totally forget about other all VR football games.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your feedback, GregzVR_! We are going to pass this feedback along to the developers. Follow us at @CityLightsVR for updates.

reviews.review_title: Good game but room for improvement.
reviews.review_description: I really like 2MD football but have been waiting for a more full experience.  MVP is close as it has full games and full seasons but it does not have some of the features that make 2MD great.  Number 1 as mentioned by all is the lack of movement by the QB.  This is particularly odd since we are playing as Mahomes who is a very mobile QB. This seems like it should be an easy add since you can move WRs and RBs AFTER they receive the ball.  An option to call plays on defense would also be a great feature to add.  The player models are really good as well as the animations.  As some have mentioned, an option to silence the arcade sounds would be great.  Lastly, if possible any adjustment to the crowd pixels would add immersion as I like the stadiums, particularly the Professional ones.  Please continue to update as you have a potential winner with a few tweaks to fill in the missing gaps.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback. We have been hearing that people want to move outside of the pocket and that the crowd should be updated. We will pass this along to the developers. Please follow us on Twitter for update news: @MVPFootballVR and @CityLightsVR.

reviews.review_description: I still love the football implementation of VR Sports Challenge (2016) more, eventhough this game has full games. Didn't like the arcade/retro sound and visual special effects. I felt my playing performance doesn't really matter that much. Don't get me wrong, the game is well made but quite quickly I didn't feel like playing again. Not my cup of tea, I guess.

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear this wasn't your favorite game! We are making updates and hope you'll give it a try again!

reviews.review_description: I love the game I would like to see multiplayer and please fix the run game and the tackling. Runningback averages one or less yards per run. Still a great game

developer_response.body: We have heard the call for multiplayer mode from lots of people. We hear ya! Thanks for the suggestion and stay tuned for updates!

reviews.review_description: It gets kinda lonely on the side line…. Maybe add team players instead of dogs, and maybe add a coin toss…. But anyway, this is my favorite game

developer_response.body: Your favorite?! We're so glad! Thanks for the feedback. We will see what we can do ;)

reviews.review_description: I played it ONE TIME FOR 3 MINUTES. it's glitchy and not well made and my refund was denied??!?!?

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: Looks like you're outside of your two week refund window. If you would like a refund, you can email us at publishing@citylightsvr.com.

Hi Hitman - we're sorry to hear this. Have you followed the below instructions?

For Oculus Quest 2 and Quest content, you may be eligible for a refund if you used the content for less than 2 hours and you've requested a refund less than 14 days after purchase.
To request using the Oculus companion app on your phone:
1. Open the Oculus companion app on your phone.
2. Tap your profile picture in the upper left corner of your phone.
3. Tap Purchase History.
4. Tap on the content you want to request a refund for, then tap Request Refund.
5. Follow the on-screen instructions to request your refund.

developer_response.body: Thank you, Haro! We will be making a few updates soon based on feedback. Follow us to stay updated: @citylightsvr @mvpfootballvr. We appreciate you!

developer_response.body: Thank you!
